messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Lease aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve. Use discriminated unions for Command, Event, and State, and throw a typed DomainError when signing an already-active lease.

Write a pure Decider for an Apartment aggregate that tracks listing, reservation, and occupancy. Include an exhaustive decide and evolve with a never default. Reject reserving an already-occupied apartment with a typed error.

Explain briefly how a Decider differs from a plain reducer, then show a working typed implementation for a MaintenanceTicket aggregate with open, assign, and resolve commands. Keep the prose outside the code fence.
Write a Decider for a RentalAgreement aggregate. Constraints: strict types, no any, typed DomainError, and the decide must reject renewal of a terminated agreement.
What's the v5-style way to build a Decider for an Amenity aggregate (gym, pool, sauna) with add and retire commands? Show a minimal example.
Build a Decider for a HousingUnit aggregate in TypeScript covering register, renovate, and decommission commands. Use typed discriminated unions and a never default in both decide and evolve.
Build a Projection / read model that aggregates BuildingBlock events into a flat occupancy summary view. Include emptyView and a project function that ignores events it does not care about.
How would I build a read model for Amenity usage counts from a stream of events? Show a minimal TypeScript example with project and emptyView.
Explain briefly what a Projection is in event sourcing, then implement one that builds a MaintenanceTicket backlog view. Prose must be outside the code fence.

Write a TypeScript projection that maintains a per-Lessee view of active leases and outstanding balance. Constraints: denormalised view type, pure project function, and ignore events outside the projection's interest.
Build a versioned Snapshot loader for a Lease aggregate. Support upgrading from v1 and v2 to the current v3 schema. Use a CURRENT_VERSION constant and sequential upgrader functions.
How do I write a Snapshot with versioned upgrade for a UtilityMeter aggregate? Give a short TypeScript example with two upgraders.
Build an optimistic concurrency write path for a Lease stream using expectedRevision. Include a local EventStore interface and a typed ConcurrencyConflict error.
Show me the optimistic-concurrency equivalent of a plain append for an Apartment stream. Use a bigint expectedRevision and surface a typed conflict.
Build a retry loop around an optimistic-concurrency append for a LeasePayment stream. On ConcurrencyConflict, reload state and retry up to 3 times. Use Effect.gen to wrap the IO.

Build a CommandHandler for the Lease aggregate that loads state from the stream, calls decide, and appends events with the expected revision. Use a local EventStore interface; no external client imports.
Explain briefly the shape of a CommandHandler over an EventStore, then implement one for MaintenanceTicket commands. Keep prose outside the fence.
Build an Effect-wrapped CommandHandler for an Apartment aggregate. Use Effect.gen, a local EventStore interface, and typed events.
Write a CommandHandler<RentalAgreementCommand> implementation with strict types. Constraints: no any, expected-revision append, local EventStore interface, and a typed ConcurrencyConflict.
Build a subscription runner that resumes a BuildingBlock projection from the last persisted checkpoint. Include a local CheckpointStore interface and write the checkpoint after applying each event.
How do I write a crash-safe subscription-from-checkpoint for a UtilityMeter projection? Show a minimal TypeScript example.
Build an Effect-wrapped subscription runner for a LeasePayment projection that resumes from a checkpoint. Use Effect.gen and a local CheckpointStore interface.
Build a generic aggregateStream helper that folds an AsyncIterable<E> into state S using a pure evolve. Then use it to rebuild a Lease aggregate.
Use a when-style reducer (Partial<A>) to replay TenantInspection events with aggregateStream and validate the final shape.
Explain briefly how aggregateStream replays a stream into state, then show a typed TypeScript implementation that folds Apartment events.

Build a chunked rebuild loop for a BuildingBlock read model. Accept a from cursor, batch size, a project, and a persist callback. Advance the cursor after each batch persists so crashes are resumable.
Write a resumable rebuild loop for a MaintenanceTicket read model with progress reporting. Constraints: no any, typed EventStore, and a from-option to resume mid-rebuild.

Build a minimal CQRS boundary for Amenity: a handleCommand(cmd) that writes via decide + appendToStream and a queryView(id) that reads from a separate read store. Note that the read side is eventually consistent.
Explain briefly why a CQRS boundary separates the read and write state types, then implement a typed handleCommand/queryView pair for HousingUnit. Keep prose outside the fence.

Build a transactional outbox append for Apartment events: inside one unit of work, append events to the stream and insert rows into an outbox table. A separate publisher polls and marks them published.
Build a TypeScript Decider for a LeasePayment aggregate with schedule, settle, and refund commands using Effect.gen for the IO wrapping. Keep evolve and decide pure.
What's the idiomatic way to fold a batch of TenantInspection events using a pure when reducer with discriminated events? Show a short example.
